pr.title: Match ef_061D 100% and rename to efspecial (special effects)
pr.author: hourianto

summary: Attempted to fully match the effect function at 0x80061D70, rename it from ef_80061D70/ef_061D toward efSync_SpawnSpecial/efspecial, and update related effect symbols. The PR rewrote the large special-effect spawn switch to use direct va_arg reads, named constants, Fighter/FighterBone accessors, JObj setters/getters, and explicit local symbol names. It was closed unmerged because reviewers said the function had already been matched upstream and this version did not improve code quality; the rename was suggested as a separate PR.
what_changed: Renamed the global symbol at .text:0x80061D70 from ef_80061D70 to efSync_SpawnSpecial in config/GALE01/symbols.txt.
Reworked the large special-effect spawn function signature from ef_80061D70(s32 gfx_id, HSD_GObj* gobj, va_list vlist) to efSync_SpawnSpecial(s32 gfx_id, HSD_GObj* arg_gobj, void* vlist).
Replaced the local EF061D_VA_ARG macro and vlist_arg temporary with direct va_arg(vlist, type) usage throughout the switch.
Added includes for math.h, baselib/class.h, baselib/debug.h, baselib/list.h, baselib/object.h, baselib/pobj.h, dolphin/mtx.h, and ft/inlines.h to support a more complete C version.
Introduced or named local data/sdata/sdata2 symbols such as ef_803BF9D0, ef_804D39D8, ef_804D39E0, ef_804D81D0, ef_804D81D8, ef_804D81E0, ef_804D81E8, ef_804D81EC, and ef_804D81F0.
Added a custom efSpecial_HSD_ASSERT_MSG macro and temporarily redefined HSD_ASSERT before including baselib/jobj.h so inlined JObj assertions would use original jobj.h file/assert strings.

Changed many effect-spawn cases to use Effect* temporaries, HSD_JObj* temporaries, GET_JOBJ, GET_FIGHTER, FighterBone arrays, and named rotation/scale constants instead of raw local casts and literals.
Updated references in efsync.c, efasync.c, and the ef_061D header to align with the proposed efSync_SpawnSpecial naming.
smart_moves: Preserved exact float/double constant placement by giving anonymous local constants stable ef_804D81xx symbol names rather than relying only on literals.
Used named baselib and fighter accessors such as GET_JOBJ and GET_FIGHTER, which made some object graph accesses clearer than raw user_data offset arithmetic.
Moved from a custom vararg extraction macro toward ordinary va_arg calls, reducing a local decomp-only abstraction.
Kept evidence of matching discipline: the author reported the final game file had the same hash and that local tooling considered it clean.
Identified that jobj.h inlines and their assertion strings were central to matching this function, even though the chosen macro override approach was later criticized.
decomp_lessons: A 100% matching rewrite is not sufficient if it makes the C source more brittle or uglier than existing/upstream code; code quality and maintainability still matter.
If a function has already been matched upstream, prefer syncing or submitting only the remaining rename/cleanup rather than carrying a competing large rewrite.
Do not redefine inline behavior from headers when the match issue indicates a real inline exists; include and use the original inline where possible.
Matching assertion string/file behavior from inlined headers can be tempting to solve with HSD_ASSERT macro hacks, but reviewers may reject that as too invasive.
Large effect switch functions benefit from isolating semantic renames from mechanical matching changes so reviewers can accept naming improvements independently.
When using AI/tooling-generated decomp output, explicitly audit for brittle macros, duplicated inlines, and local hacks before review.
naming_conventions: Function at 0x80061D70 was proposed as efSync_SpawnSpecial, matching the existing efSync_Spawn naming pattern and the PR title's 'special effects' interpretation.
Local data symbols in the effect module used ef_ address-style names, e.g. ef_803BF9D0 for the JOBJ_USE_QUATERNION assertion string.
Effect module constants in .sdata2 were named by address, e.g. ef_804D81D0 through ef_804D81F0, where semantic names were not established.
Temporary variables in the rewrite used numbered role names such as eff_1, eff_2, jobj_1, jobj_2, f64_1, and f32_1; this matched mechanically but did not add much semantic clarity.
Reviewer feedback implied that file/module rename from ef_061D to efspecial should be handled separately from matching changes.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Direct va_arg(vlist, T) ordering was used to reproduce switch-case argument consumption and replace a custom EF061D_VA_ARG wrapper.
A manual PAD_STACK(0x98) plus explicit stack padding locals such as sp_pad128, sp130, and sp_pad130 were used to control stack layout.
Float sign tests selected between two double constants, then narrowed through a f32 temporary before calling HSD_JObjSetRotationY, preserving the original conversion pattern.
Extern address-named constants were used for 0.0f and +/- pi-over-two style values rather than embedding all literals directly.
The rewrite relied on JObj inline setters/getters such as HSD_JObjSetRotationY, HSD_JObjSetRotationZ, HSD_JObjSetScaleX/Y/Z, HSD_JObjGetScaleY, HSD_JObjGetChild, and HSD_JObjGetTranslationZ.
Custom HSD_ASSERT redefinition around baselib/jobj.h attempted to force inlined JObj assertion metadata to match original strings and line numbers.

review_feedback.author: PsiLupan
review_feedback.feedback: The custom/redefined code was indicative of an inline from jobj.h and should not require redefining it.

review_feedback.author: hourianto
review_feedback.feedback: The author acknowledged the macro hackery with defines/includes at the top of the file was ugly and brittle, and said they would see whether using the inlines was now stable enough.

review_feedback.author: ribbanya
review_feedback.feedback: The PR did not improve code quality for the function, the function had already been matched upstream, and the file rename should be submitted separately.

review_feedback.author: unknown/review excerpt
review_feedback.feedback: A TODO comment was added; reviewer/author noted the inline-related cleanup did not seem straightforward.

follow_up_queries: Find the upstream PR or commit that matched ef_80061D70 / efSync_SpawnSpecial before this PR was closed.
Search for a later PR that only renames ef_061D.c to efspecial.c or ef_061D.h to efspecial.h.
Inspect baselib/jobj.h inlines used by HSD_JObjSetRotationY/SetScale/GetChild to avoid redefining HSD_ASSERT in future matching work.
Look for accepted patterns in other modules for matching inline assertion file strings without macro hackery.
